geopolitics_summary: Strait of Hormuz closure since late February sustains LNG supply risk; EU gas prices diverging from US as Middle East disruption constrains global LNG.

themes.headline: International LNG prices rise amid Strait of Hormuz closure
themes.why_it_matters: Hormuz closure restricts Middle East LNG exports to Europe; TTF/Asian arb widens, supporting EU gas premiums and marginal power costs in gas-weighted hubs.

themes.headline: DOE released 17.5 million barrels from SPR since March
themes.why_it_matters: Large US SPR releases suppress crude prices; lower oil feedstock costs ease global LNG production, increasing supply to Europe and capping TTF upside.

themes.headline: US renewable diesel and SAF exports reach 50k b/d in 2H25
themes.why_it_matters: US biofuel exports reduce crude demand slightly; modest indirect downward pressure on crude prices supports global LNG competitiveness vs. EU renewables switching.

watchlist: Monitor Strait of Hormuz reopening risk; any resolution would immediately ease LNG supply to EU.
Track SPR drawdown pace; accelerated releases could further weaken crude/LNG price support.
Watch US-EU LNG arb spread (TTF vs Henry Hub); widening confirms supply bottleneck.
